title: Evax asterisciflora
taxonomy: Evax asterisciflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region. The classification of Evax asterisciflora includes its kingdom as Plantae and its order as Asterales. Further classification places it in the genus Evax, which is a small genus of annual or biennial plants. Evax asterisciflora is closely related to other plants in the same family, particularly those in the genera Helichrysum and Gnaphalium.

intro: Evax asterisciflora, also known as Asteriscus maritimus, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly found in the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments, including digestive issues and infections. The bioactive constituents of Evax asterisciflora include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Evax asterisciflora is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to alleviate digestive problems and promote overall well-being.
